7.1.title: 7-1: Slope Fields & Differential Equations
7.1.content:
FUN-7 · Modeling

What is a differential equation?

An equation involving a derivative of an unknown function, e.g. dy/dx = f(x,y). A solution is a function whose derivative satisfies the equation on an interval.

Slope fields

At each grid point (x,y), draw a short segment with slope = dy/dx from the DE. Solution curves follow the tangents. Use the field to sketch families of solutions and to reason about long-run behavior.

Equilibrium & stability (concept)

Constant solutions occur where dy/dx = 0. Nearby solutions may approach or leave those values — connects to real modeling (populations, cooling, etc.).

AP tip: Match a given slope field to a DE by testing slopes at a few labeled points.

7.2.title: 7-2: Separation of Variables & Exponential Models
7.2.content:
FUN-7 · Solving

Separation of variables

If dy/dx = g(x)h(y), rewrite as (1/h(y)) dy = g(x) dx and integrate both sides. Solve for y if possible; use initial conditions to fix +C.

Exponential growth/decay

dP/dt = kP ⇒ P(t) = P₀ ekt

k > 0 growth; k < 0 decay. Logistic models add a carrying capacity — watch for that in BC-style prompts.

Domain of solutions

Watch values that make you divide by zero in the separated form; solutions may only exist on restricted intervals.

AP tip: Show separation and both integrals explicitly on FRQs; substitute initial values to find C.

flashcards.front: Differential equation: what is a solution?
flashcards.back: A function (or family) that satisfies the DE on an interval.
Algebraically isolate each variable on opposite sides, then integrate.
